  "decision": "fail",

  "percent_complete": 50,

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Unprotected trench depth is 5 feet or greater with workers actively inside",
    "Lack of cave-in protection system (sloping, benching, shielding, or shoring)"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Order an immediate stop to all work inside the trench",
    "Evacuate all workers from the unprotected trench immediately",
    "Install an engineered protective system (such as a trench box or shoring) or slope the excavation before allowing re-entry",
    "Ensure a competent person inspects the trench and protective systems prior to resuming work"
  ],
  "rationale": "Under OSHA 1926 Subpart P, any trench 5 feet or deeper requires an adequate protective system to prevent cave-ins. Allowing workers inside an unprotected trench of this depth poses an immediate, life-threatening hazard (critical risk) and requires an immediate work stoppage.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926.652(a)(1)"
  ]
}
